[{"scrollable-horizontal": false, "draw": false, "ancestors": ["android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "clickable": false, "pressed": "not_pressed", "text": " 
  Chief complaint: 
 Shortness Of Breath and wheezing for 1/2 h. 
 
 History of presenting illness:  
 A 11-year-old child is taken to the emergency department because he is feeling short of breath. The episode began about an hour previously while the child was playing sports, it was sever that preventing him from playing , not relieved by the rest . he also abruptly developed paroxysms of wheezing ,chest tightness and continues dry cough ,Exercise (quantify distance to breathlessness). Days per week off work or school? 1-2
 Diurnal variation? more at evening and early morning. 
 Precipitating factors: emotion, exercise, infection, allergens and drugs.
 Any other atopic diseases like eczema, hay fever, allergy.
 Any Family history of asthma?
 Any Acid reflux? Occupational history?
 
 Review of other system:  
 
 CVS system : 
 normal 
 GIT: 
 normal 
 Renal system : 
 normal 
 CNS: 
 normal 
 Past medical Hx:  
 There is same previous attacks occur mainly at the cold weather and during exercise not sever like this ,resolve spontaneously without Dr. visit There is no HX of atopic dermatitis He have positive HX of hay fever 
 Past surgical Hx:  
 No any surgical operation , no any trauma
 
 Drug Hx:  
 No previous drug taking He is allergic to the penicillin 
 
 Family Hex:  
 There is HX of atopic dermatitis in his one brother , other to with the father having HX of hay fever 
 
 Social Hex: 
 Medium socio-economic status , There is animal in the house His father is smoker.

[{"scrollable-horizontal": false, "draw": false, "ancestors": ["android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "clickable": false, "pressed": "not_pressed", "text": " 
 Physical examination  
 
 Vital signs: 
 •Pulse rate: 160 bin 
 •Blood pressure: 1100 
 •Respiratory rate: 40 min 
 •Temperature : 37.5 C 
 
 General Examination: 
 11-year old male child , looks ill, is sitting leaning forward and is using his accessory respiratory muscles confused, not anemic , not jaundiced , and there is no abnormal movement , There is no finger clubbing, No leg edema . 
 Systemic examination:  
 Respiratory examination  
 A. 
 Inspection:. Accessory muscles use ,No scar, no dilated vein no visible pulsation , normal chest shape no Harrison sulcus 
 Palpation: 
 Trachea is central
 Chest movement 3cm. 
 Equal Vocal resonance 
 
 Percussion: 
 Resonant equal in both sites 
 
 Auscultation: prolonged expiratory phase with relatively high-pitched wheezes through much of the respiratory cycle are heard. No fine crackles are heard.

 DDX: 
 Asthma
 Bronchiectasis 
 Pneumonia
 Pulmonary edema
 Pulmonary embolus
 
 Diagnosis : 
 This patient most likely has asthma based on the initial presentation. Asthma is characterized by reversible airway obstruction, airway inflammation, and bronchospasm of the airways in response to a variety of stimuli.
